summary: Glenwood Springs' verified affordable-housing brief now rests on direct URL fetches for Ballot Measure 2C, recent 2C appropriations, CHFA tax-credit awards, the Strategic Housing Plan Update, city-owned land projects, WMRHC / Good Deeds, and Garfield County co-investments tied to The Benedict and Canyon Vista.

paragraphs.text: Glenwood Springs' Workforce Housing Fund was created after voters approved Ballot Measure 2C, a 2.5% accommodations tax on overnight stays at hotels, motels, short-term rentals, and similar lodging. City materials forecast about $1.7 million per year from the tax, while the original September 1, 2022 city release said the council referred the question to the November 8, 2022 ballot.

The fund is restricted to workforce housing: Post Independent reporting says it can be used only for workforce housing development for a person working within city limits or the 81601 ZIP code, that council appropriates the funds, and that expenditures are subject to an annual public audit. The city's advisory-board page says the WHFAB was commissioned by council in 2023 to help manage the fund and advise council on 2C-funded workforce-housing programs.

Recent direct-fetched 2C-related appropriations include the $3.8 million Canyon Vista package approved in December 2024, including earlier city commitments and fee backfills; a $3 million city package for Habitat for Humanity in exchange for 15 priority units; and a three-year $300,000 commitment to WMRHC's Good Deeds program.

CHFA's 2023 Round One applicant report lists Benedict at 2800 Midland Avenue in Glenwood Springs, requesting $1,449,710 in 9% credits for 34 low-income senior units, with Archdiocesan Housing as owner.

CHFA's 2024 Round Two announcement lists Glenwood Gardens in Glenwood Springs with Cohen-Esrey Development Group as developer. Local reporting describes the same former Glenwood Gardens site as Canyon Vista, an 80-unit affordable project between U.S. Highway 6 and Donegan Road serving residents earning 20% to 80% of AMI, and notes a $1.3 million Proposition 123 Concessionary Debt award.

The city says council adopted the Strategic Housing Plan Update in July 2023. The plan summary identifies approximately 1,491 cost-burdened Glenwood households, 38% of households, and says the city needed 1,346 new units in 2023 to reduce cost burden by 50%, fill unfilled jobs, and reduce in-commuting from 78% to 70%; the need rises to 1,858 units by 2043.

The full Strategic Housing Plan Update explains the Proposition 123 implementation rule that municipalities must establish a plan to increase affordable housing inventory by 3% annually starting in 2027.

Post Independent coverage says the city pursued two city-owned parcels for Habitat for Humanity affordable housing: the Eighth Street site and the Iddings property on Airport Road. Later coverage says Habitat and the city were working on two developments, one at the Eighth Street site and one at Airport Road.

For Vogelaar Park, Post Independent reporting says Glenwood Springs was looking to build an estimated 100 to 150 workforce units at Eighth and School streets and that Garfield County commissioners approved a support letter for a DOLA More Housing Now and Land Use Initiative grant. The city project page says a portion of the current baseball-diamond area would be used primarily for housing with possible limited commercial or retail space.

sections.heading: West Mountain Regional Housing Coalition and Good Deeds

paragraphs.text: Glenwood Springs is listed as a member of the West Mountain Regional Housing Coalition. Direct-fetched sources describe WMRHC as a Colorado 501(c)(3) serving the Roaring Fork and Colorado River valleys, and Sopris Sun reporting says RFTA joined the coalition for $20,000 per year after most RFTA member governments, including Glenwood Springs, were already coalition members.

The city committed $200,000 to WMRHC's Good Deeds buy-down program in 2024 and approved a three-year, $300,000 commitment in 2025. Post Independent reporting describes Good Deeds as a program that buys down part of a home purchase in exchange for a permanent deed restriction, with Glenwood Springs contributing to homes for local workers.

Garfield County Housing Authority reporting says seven project-based vouchers were used in 2025 for The Benedict in Glenwood Springs and that projected 2026-2027 allocations include 20 project-based vouchers for Canyon Vista in Glenwood Springs.

Garfield County's Canyon Vista release says DOLA approved $3.5 million from Transformational Housing Loan Fund dollars to Cohen-Esrey for 80 apartments in Glenwood Springs. A separate county release says Garfield County transferred nearly $4.1 million in private activity bonds to CHFA and that the county's 2024 allocation was recommended for Cohen-Esrey's Canyon Vista in Glenwood Springs.
